message.content:
Babies typically start speaking single-word responses around 12 months of age.

Key milestones:

6–9 months: Babbling with repeated consonant sounds (“ba-ba,” “da-da”).

9–12 months: Babbling starts to sound more like real words; some babies may say their first meaningful words.

12–18 months: Most babies use one-word utterances consistently to name objects, people, or express needs.

18–24 months: Begin combining two words into simple phrases.

Variation is normal; some children start a bit earlier or later, depending on exposure and individual development.

If you want, I can give a practical checklist for encouraging single-word speech at this stage.
